Generate a Data Frame of Column Classes

Source: R/summarize_column_classes.R
summarize_column_classes.Rd

This function takes a list of data frames and returns a summary data frame showing the classes of each column across all the data frames. Missing columns in some data frames are represented as NA.

Usage

summarize_column_classes(all_data)

Arguments

all_data

List. A list of data frames.

Value

A data frame with rows representing the data frames and columns representing the column names from all the data frames. The values are the classes of the columns or NA if the column is missing in a data frame.

Examples

# Example usage:
all_data <- list(
    df1 = data.frame(a = 1:3, b = letters[1:3]),
    df2 = data.frame(a = 4:6, c = c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE))
)
column_classes_df <- summarize_column_classes(all_data)
print(column_classes_df)
#>   data_frame       a         b       c
#> 1        df1 integer character    <NA>
#> 2        df2 integer      <NA> logical
